mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and relaxes financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is strongest when the policy rate is already above its recent trend and turns down, because that marks an active easing impulse rather than stable policy.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS falls over a 3-month window, or if the rolling 2-year rank correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening household income and demand, which reduces earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 forward returns lower over the next month. The effect should appear after a clear labor-market deterioration, not during flat unemployment readings.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not produce negative 20-day returns more often than not, or if the average triggered return exceeds the unconditional mean by at least 1 percentage point.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand strength: stronger retail sales support revenue growth, while higher housing starts confirm cyclical expansion. Together they predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter because earnings breadth improves when consumer demand and construction activity rise in tandem.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST signal fails to outperform each single-series signal separately, or if 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 55% of joint-strength periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and improving capacity utilization, which supports cyclical earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. This signal should work best when output growth is rising from a prior slowdown rather than already at a peak.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in INDPRO are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns in a majority of cases, or if the sign of the rolling 3-year association turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in GDPC1 growth signals slower aggregate demand and weaker future corporate sales, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect is strongest when GDP has already decelerated for two consecutive quarterly observations, which marks a genuine growth slowdown rather than a noisy one-off print.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth decelerates for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: Reject if triggered GDP slowdowns are not followed by negative 60-day returns more often than positive returns, or if the average triggered return is above the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation pace, real policy becomes more accommodative and equities should rise over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism is a falling real policy rate, which supports multiples until the market fully prices the inflation surprise.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS < CPIAUCSL trend
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-rate signal does not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or if the interaction term between inflation acceleration and low policy rates is not positive in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: A continued rise in PAYEMS indicates labor-income momentum and stronger consumer spending, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week through earnings revisions and sentiment. This is a short-horizon signal because payroll changes are quickly incorporated into growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if positive PAYEMS momentum is not followed by positive 5-day returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears entirely after accounting for the prior 3-month payroll trend.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-led demand slowdown, captured by falling HOUST together with weak RSAFS, predicts lower S&P 500 returns over the next month because consumers and builders are both losing momentum. The signal fails when housing weakens but retail sales remain firm, since then the downturn is sector-specific rather than economy-wide.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS does not precede negative 20-day returns in a majority of cases, or if either proxy alone explains the outcome better than the interaction.
